title: Eulophidium paniculatum
taxonomy: Eulophidium paniculatum is classified under the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the flowering plant order Asparagales. Further, it falls under the subfamily Epidendroideae, a subfamily of orchids. The genus Eulophidium is a monotypic genus, meaning it consists of only one species, E. paniculatum. Within the Orchidaceae family, E. paniculatum is also placed under the tribe Coelogyneae.

The plant may have antioxidant properties, which could help protect cells from damage and reduce inflammation. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. Further studies are required to fully understand the potential health benefits of Eulophidium paniculatum.

It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Studies have also shown that it possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis.
